problem_statement:
We are given an ГўВҖВҳN*MГўВҖВҷ matrix. Every cell of the matrix has some chocolates
    on it, mat[i][j] gives us the number of chocolates. We have two friends ГўВҖВҳAliceГўВҖВҷ and ГўВҖВҳBobГўВҖВҷ. initially, Alice is
    standing on the cell(0,0) and Bob is standing on the cell(0, M-1). Both of them can move only to the cells below
    them in these three directions: to the bottom cell (ГўВҶВ“), to the bottom-right cell(ГўВҶВҳ), or to the bottom-left cell(ГўВҶВҷ).

    When Alica and Bob visit a cell, they take all the chocolates from that cell with them. It can happen that they
    visit the same cell, in that case, the chocolates need to be considered only once.

    They cannot go out of the boundary of the given matrix, we need to return the maximum number of chocolates that Bob
    and Alice can together collect.
